| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Development Environment
| MCP:y9W8X9 | SPRING_PROFILES_ACTIVE=dev
| MCP:y9W8X9 | SPRING_DATASOURCE_URL=jdbc:postgresql://db:5432/spring_template_dev
| MCP:y9W8X9 | POSTGRES_DB=spring_template_dev
| MCP:y9W8X9 | POSTGRES_USER=development
| MCP:y9W8X9 | POSTGRES_PASSWORD=T3st!ng
| MCP:y9W8X9 |

| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Spring Boot Template
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | A production-ready template for Spring Boot projects with modern best practices and built-in infrastructure.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ![Docker Compose](https://imgur.com/qWLYDrg.png)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ![Swagger UI](https://imgur.com/5MixfZe.png)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Features
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Spring Boot 4.0.0** (Java 25)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **UUID Primary Keys** via Hibernate `@UuidGenerator`
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **JWT Authentication & Authorization** with refresh tokens
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Role-based Security** with Spring Security
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Spring Boot Actuator** for health, info, and metrics endpoints
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Request Correlation ID** — automatic `X-Correlation-ID` header propagation and MDC logging
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Structured JSON Logging** — human-readable console + JSON file output (Logstash format)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Pagination** — built into `BaseService` and controllers via Spring Data `Pageable`
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Rate Limiting** — annotation-based (`@RateLimit`, `@RateLimitCategory`) with configurable categories
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Idempotency** — `@Idempotent` annotation with Redis-backed request deduplication
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Swagger UI** for API documentation ([http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html))
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **PostgreSQL**, **Redis**, **Kafka & Zookeeper** — all containerized via Docker Compose
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Multi-stage Dockerfile** with dependency caching and JRE runtime
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **JaCoCo Code Coverage** reports
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Maven Enforcer** — ensures Java 25+ and Maven 3.8.7+
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Lombok** and **MapStruct** for reduced boilerplate
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Environment Isolation** — run dev, test, and prod in parallel via Docker Compose
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - **Unit & Integration Tests** for services, controllers, and infrastructure
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | > **Note:** Java 25 and Maven 3.8.7+ are required.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Quick Start
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ### 1. Use This Template
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Click **"Use this template"** on GitHub, then clone your new repository:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/<your-repo>.git
| MCP:y9W8X9 | cd <your-repo>
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ### 2. Initialize Your Project
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Run the initialization script to rename packages, modules, and containers to match your project:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ./init-project.sh --group-id com.acme --artifact my-app --name "My App" --author "Your Name"
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | Option | Description | Required |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | |--------|-------------|----------|
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `--group-id` | Maven groupId / Java package root (e.g. `com.acme`) | Yes |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `--artifact` | Artifact name in kebab-case (e.g. `my-app`) | Yes |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `--name` | Human-readable project name (default: derived from artifact) | No |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `--author` | Author name for `@author` tags (default: `tzesh`) | No |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | You can also run it interactively — omit the flags and you'll be prompted:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ./init-project.sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | The script will:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Rename Java packages (`com.tzesh.springtemplate` -> your package)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Rename module directories (`spring-boot-template-api` -> `<artifact>-api`, etc.)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Update Docker container names, Kafka group IDs, and all references
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Replace `@author` tags if `--author` is provided
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Self-delete after completion
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ### 3. Verify the setup
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ./mvnw clean install
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ### 4. Start infrastructure and run
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Start PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka
| MCP:y9W8X9 | docker compose up -d db redis zookeeper kafka
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Run the application
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ./mvnw -pl spring-boot-template-api spring-boot:run
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Or run everything with Docker:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | docker compose up -d
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | The application will be available at [http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html](http://localhost:8080/api/v1/swagger-ui/index.html).
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Project Structure
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | SpringBootTemplate/
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ├── pom.xml                          # Parent POM (enforcer, JaCoCo, shared deps)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ├── spring-boot-template-core/       # Core module (base classes, annotations, exceptions)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │   └── src/main/java/.../base/
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── annotation/              # @RateLimit, @Idempotent, etc.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── dto/                     # BaseDTO
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── entity/                  # BaseEntity
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── exception/               # NotFoundException, RateLimitExceededException, etc.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── handler/                 # @ControllerAdvice exception handlers
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── mapper/                  # BaseMapper (MapStruct)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── response/                # BaseResponse wrapper
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       └── service/                 # BaseService (CRUD + pagination)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ├── spring-boot-template-api/        # API module (controllers, services, config)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │   ├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage build
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │   └── src/main/java/.../
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── config/
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       │   ├── correlation/         # CorrelationIdFilter
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       │   ├── idempotency/         # IdempotencyAspect
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       │   ├── ratelimit/           # RateLimitAspect, RateLimitingFilter
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       │   └── security/            # SecurityChain, JWT filters
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       ├── controller/              # REST controllers
| MCP:y9W8X9 | │       └── service/                 # Business services
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ├── docker-compose.yml               # PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka, app
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ├── init-project.sh                  # Project initialization script
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ├── .editorconfig                    # Editor formatting rules
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ├── .gitattributes                   # Git line ending config
| MCP:y9W8X9 | └── .dockerignore                    # Docker build exclusions
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## API Usage
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 1. **Register** a new user via `POST /auth/register`
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 2. **Login** via `POST /auth/login` to obtain a JWT token
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 3. **Authorize** in Swagger UI using the `Authorize` button
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 4. **Access** secured endpoints (e.g. user management requires `ADMIN` role)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Actuator Endpoints
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Health, info, and metrics are exposed at:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | Endpoint | Description |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | |----------|-------------|
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/health` | Application health status |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/info` | Application info |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `GET /api/v1/actuator/metrics` | Application metrics |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Health details are shown when authenticated with an authorized role. Kubernetes liveness and readiness probes are enabled.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Correlation ID
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Every request is assigned a correlation ID for distributed tracing:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - If the client sends an `X-Correlation-ID` header, it is reused
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Otherwise, a UUID is generated automatically
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - The correlation ID is added to the MDC for all log output and returned in the response header
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Rate Limiting
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ### Annotation-based (per-endpoint)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```java
| MCP:y9W8X9 | @RateLimit(limit = 20, duration = 1, key = RateLimitKeyStrategy.USER)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | @RateLimitCategory(RateLimitCategoryType.STANDARD)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ### Configurable categories in `application.properties`
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | Category | Default Limit | Duration |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | |----------|--------------|----------|
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `strict` | 10 req | 1 min |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `standard` | 60 req | 1 min |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `relaxed` | 200 req | 1 min |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | | `authentication` | 5 req | 1 min |
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Idempotency
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Annotate mutating endpoints with `@Idempotent` to prevent duplicate processing. Clients must send an `Idempotency-Key` header. Backed by Redis.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```java
| MCP:y9W8X9 | @PostMapping("/")
| MCP:y9W8X9 | @Idempotent
| MCP:y9W8X9 | public ResponseEntity<BaseResponse<UserDTO>> createUser(...) { ... }
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Kafka Messaging
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Kafka and Zookeeper are included in Docker Compose
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - `POST /kafka/send` sends messages to Kafka (supports optional scheduled delivery)
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Consumer logs received messages
| MCP:y9W8X9 | - Logging level configurable in `application.properties`
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Environment Configuration
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | Use `.env` files for environment-specific variables and Docker Compose override files for port/resource separation:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```sh
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Run test environment
| MCP:y9W8X9 | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.test.override.yml --env-file .env.test -p myapp_test up -d
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Run prod environment
| MCP:y9W8X9 | docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.prod.override.yml --env-file .env.prod -p myapp_prod up -d
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Code Coverage
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | JaCoCo reports are generated during `mvn test` and available at:
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring-boot-template-api/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring-boot-template-core/target/site/jacoco/index.html
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ```
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Contributing
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for setup instructions, code style, and PR process.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ---
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## License
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | See [LICENSE](LICENSE) for details.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | successfully downloaded text file (SHA: b9ba3257cb20e556b731916d2f904e022bae9b4a)

| MCP:y9W8X9 | ## Base Configuration File for Spring Boot Template API
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # ==========================
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # This file contains the base configuration for the Spring Boot Template API.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # It includes settings for server, application, database, JPA, and JWT.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # ==========================
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Note: This file is intended to be used as a template. Modify the values as needed for your environment.
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # ==========================
| MCP:y9W8X9 |

| MCP:y9W8X9 | server.servlet.context-path=/api/v1/

| MCP:y9W8X9 | application.version=2.0
| MCP:y9W8X9 |

| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=update
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # JWT Configuration
| MCP:y9W8X9 | security.jwt.secret-key=34743777217A25432A462D4A614E645267556B58703273357638782F413F4428472B4B6250655368566D597133743677397A244226452948404D635166546A57
| MCP:y9W8X9 | security.jwt.authorization-key=614E645267556B58703273357638792F423F4528482B4B6250655368566D597133743677397A24432646294A404E635166546A576E5A7234753778214125442A
| MCP:y9W8X9 | security.jwt.expiration=86400000
| MCP:y9W8X9 | security.jwt.refresh-expiration=604800000
| MCP:y9W8X9 | security.jwt.issuer=SpringTemplate
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Rate Limit Configuration
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ratelimit.enabled=true

| MCP:y9W8X9 | ratelimit.standard.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ratelimit.relaxed.limit=200
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ratelimit.relaxed.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ratelimit.authentication.limit=5
| MCP:y9W8X9 | ratelimit.authentication.duration-minutes=1
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Redis Configuration
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.data.redis.host=redis
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.data.redis.port=6379
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Spring Session with Redis
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.session.store-type=redis
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.session.redis.namespace=spring:session
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.session.timeout=30m
| MCP:y9W8X9 | 
| MCP:y9W8X9 | # Kafka Configuration
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.kafka.bootstrap-servers=kafka:9092
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.kafka.consumer.group-id=spring-template-group
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.kafka.consumer.auto-offset-reset=earliest
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.kafka.consumer.key-deserializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer
| MCP:y9W8X9 | spring.kafka.consumer.value-deserializer=org.apache.kafka.common.serialization.StringDeserializer

data.content:
Based on my analysis of the codebase, here's a comprehensive explanation of how this Spring Boot template handles security and protected API usage:

## Security Architecture Overview

This template implements a **JWT-based authentication system** with **role-based access control (RBAC)** and several additional security layers. The architecture is designed to be production-ready with stateless authentication.

---

## 1. JWT Authentication

### Token Generation & Validation (`JwtService.java`)

The JWT system uses the **JJWT library** with HMAC-SHA signing keys:

- **Secret Key**: Configured via `security.jwt.secret-key` in `application.properties` (Base64-encoded)
- **Access Token Expiration**: 24 hours (`security.jwt.expiration=86400000` ms)
- **Refresh Token Expiration**: 7 days (`security.jwt.refresh-expiration=604800000` ms)
- **Issuer**: Configurable via `security.jwt.issuer` (default: "SpringTemplate")

The service extracts the username from the token subject, validates tokens against `UserDetails`, and checks expiration.

### Authentication Filter (`AuthenticationFilter.java`)

A `OncePerRequestFilter` that:

1. **Bypasses auth routes**: Requests to `/api/v1/auth` skip the filter entirely
2. **Extracts Bearer token**: Looks for `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header
3. **Validates token**: Checks if the token is valid (not expired, matches user) AND not revoked in the database
4. **Sets SecurityContext**: Creates a `UsernamePasswordAuthenticationToken` with user authorities

### Token Storage & Revocation (`TokenEntity.java`, `TokenRepository.java`)

Tokens are persisted in the database with revocation flags:
- Each login generates a new token stored in `TOKEN` table
- Previous tokens are revoked on new login (`revokeAllUserTokens`)
- Tokens can be individually marked as `expired` or `revoked`
- Token validation checks both JWT validity AND database revocation status

---

## 2. Role-Based Access Control (RBAC)

### Roles (`Role.java`)

Three roles with hierarchical permissions:

| Role | Permissions |
|------|------------|
| **USER** | None (default role on registration) |
| **MANAGER** | `MANAGER_READ`, `MANAGER_UPDATE`, `MANAGER_DELETE`, `MANAGER_CREATE` |
| **ADMIN** | All MANAGER permissions + `ADMIN_READ`, `ADMIN_UPDATE`, `ADMIN_DELETE`, `ADMIN_CREATE` |

### Permissions (`Permission.java`)

Eight granular permissions following the pattern `{scope}:{action}`:
- `admin:read`, `admin:update`, `admin:create`, `admin:delete`
- `management:read`, `management:update`, `management:create`, `management:delete`

---

## 3. Security Filter Chain (`SecurityChain.java`)

The security configuration defines:

### Public Endpoints (no authentication required)
- `/auth/**` - Authentication routes (register, login, refresh, authorize)
- `/v3/api-docs/**`, `/swagger-ui/**`, `/webjars/**` - API documentation
- `/actuator/**` - Health and monitoring endpoints

### Protected Endpoints with Role Enforcement

**Management endpoints** (`/management/**`) have layered restrictions:

```java
// Requires ADMIN or MANAGER role
.requestMatchers("/management/**").hasAnyRole(ADMIN.name(), MANAGER.name())

// HTTP method-specific permission checks:
GET    → admin:read OR management:read
POST   → admin:create OR management:create
PUT    → admin:update OR management:update
PATCH  → admin:update OR management:update
DELETE → admin:delete OR management:delete
```

**All other endpoints** require authentication but no specific role.

### Session Management
- **Stateless**: `SessionCreationPolicy.STATELESS` - no server-side sessions
- CSRF disabled (typical for JWT-based APIs)

---

## 4. Authentication Flow

### Registration (`POST /auth/register`)
1. Validates unique username/email
2. Hashes password with **BCrypt**
3. Creates user with `USER` role (default)
4. Generates access + refresh tokens
5. Stores access token in database

### Login (`POST /auth/login`)
1. Authenticates credentials via Spring's `AuthenticationManager`
2. Revokes all previous tokens for the user
3. Generates new access + refresh tokens
4. Stores access token in database

### Token Refresh (`POST /auth/refresh-token`)
1. Extracts refresh token from `Authorization` header
2. Validates refresh token (not expired, matches user)
3. Generates new access token
4. Revokes old tokens, stores new one

### Role Authorization (`POST /auth/authorize`)
- Requires an **authorization key** (configured via `security.jwt.authorization-key`)
- Allows promoting users to `ADMIN` or `MANAGER` roles
- Protected by `@PreAuthorize("hasRole('ADMIN') || hasRole('USER') || hasRole('MODERATOR')")`
- Uses idempotency protection (Redis-backed)

---

## 5. Additional Security Layers

### Rate Limiting
- **Authentication endpoints**: 5 requests/minute
- **Standard endpoints**: 60 requests/minute
- **Strict endpoints**: 10 requests/minute
- Configurable per-endpoint with `@RateLimit` annotation

### Idempotency Protection
- `@Idempotent` annotation prevents duplicate request processing
- Uses `Idempotency-Key` header backed by Redis
- Applied to registration endpoint

### Authentication Error Handling (`AuthenticationEntryPoint.java`)
Returns structured JSON responses with:
- HTTP 401 status
- Error message "Unauthorized to access this resource"
- Request path for debugging

---

## 6. User Interaction with Secured Endpoints

### Using Swagger UI
1. **Register**: `POST /auth/register` with username, email, password, name
2. **Login**: `POST /auth/login` with username, password → receive JWT tokens
3. **Authorize**: Click "Authorize" button in Swagger UI → paste `Bearer <access_token>`
4. **Access endpoints**: All subsequent requests include the JWT automatically

### API Client Usage
```bash
# 1. Register
curl -X POST /auth/register -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"user1", "email":"user1@example.com", "password":"pass", "name":"User 1"}'

# 2. Login
curl -X POST /auth/login -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"user1", "password":"pass"}'
# Response: {"accessToken": "eyJ...", "refreshToken": "eyJ..."}

# 3. Access protected endpoint
curl -X GET /management/users \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJ..."

# 4. Refresh token
curl -X POST /auth/refresh-token \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <refresh_token>"
```

### Authorization Promotion
To promote a user to ADMIN or MANAGER (requires the authorization key):
```bash
curl -X POST /auth/authorize \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username":"user1", "role":"ADMIN", "secret":"<authorization_key>"}'
```

---

## Security Best Practices Implemented

1. **Password hashing**: BCrypt with automatic salting
2. **Token rotation**: Previous tokens revoked on login
3. **Database-backed revocation**: Tokens can be invalidated server-side
4. **Rate limiting**: Prevents brute-force attacks
5. **Stateless design**: No server-side session storage
6. **Granular permissions**: Method-level authorization checks
7. **Secure headers**: Proper `Bearer` token validation
8. **Idempotency**: Prevents duplicate operations
9. **Separate authorization key**: Role changes require additional secret
